blocks.text: A South Island first — a brand-new Snow Factory is now operating at the surface conveyor lifts, producing snow in all weather, even at 20°C. The 2026 season is extended, running from 29 May to 4 October. Open for winter made early 29 May - 12 June. A reshaped Kidszone offers more space and a better learning experience for first-timers. Upgraded rental gear across Queenstown. New Safe Zones on key runs, monitored by Trail Safety to support on-snow safety. A third winch groomer joins the fleet, boosting grooming on steeper terrain and opening new areas. A new winter trail map makes mountain navigation easier than ever. Increased skibus capacity with the purchase of 3 new MAN 4WD coaches and a 4th on delivery mid-season.
experience, were guests can enjoy a day heliskiing with one of our experienced Snowsports coaches.
Coronet Peak’s family favourite! Enjoy a fun-filled day with treats, colouring, face painting, and even balloon animals from Ru. Perfect for kids and families to make memories together!
Hit the slopes for a shred down the mountain and
a boogie on the deck at an iconic Night Ski Party! Headline act is Hot Dub Time Machine.
Hit the slopes with your pooch to celebrate our 52nd Dog Derby community race.
9 Bringing together summer and winter for an epic race down the mountain on bikes – first to the bottom wins! coronetpeak.co.nz/events
WINTER MADE EARLY WITH KIDZONE CARPET UPGRADE

The Kidzone carpet has been completely remodelled, transforming the former Kaser carpet Coronet Peak is set to guarantee early winter into a dedicated beginner and kids’ area. The openings from 2026 with the installation of a new Snow Factory has enabled a larger, flatter state-of-the-art Snow Factory. learning slope (8° pitch, 80m long), with improved safety zones and easier access to the main hill. This innovative technology, a first for South The upgrade delivers a second purpose-built Island ski areas, can produce snow regardless of slope for kids’ lessons, reducing congestion and air temperature, allowing Coronet Peak to open creating a more confident and enjoyable learning on May 29, 2026 – earlier than ever before. environment for first-time skiers and riders. The Snow Factory’s $2 million-plus investment is part of a wider $4 million capital works programme at Coronet Peak this year. Located between the surface conveyor lifts area and the Kidzone Carpet, it will provide reliable snow for lower learner slopes and the new kidzone learner area. The project is on track for completion over the summer months before winter 2026. Earlier openings are expected to boost Queenstown’s winter season, benefiting local businesses and staff while attracting both beginner skiers and visitors seeking snow play experiences. coronetpeak.co.nz/mountain

Coronet Peak is launching a new Safe Zones NZSki has launched the peak of alpine adventure initiative, supported by the innovative Elevate with its SnowSports Premium Heli Experience, Engage app, to enhance on-slope safety and combining elite private coaching and exclusive responsible behaviour. heli-skiing in partnership with Southern Lakes Heliski and Alpine Group. This digital platform empowers dedicated Trail Safety and Ski Patrol teams with advanced tools, On offer is VIP amenities, priority lift access, and enabling them to proactively manage guest world-class instruction. Guests start with a full- behaviour on the mountain. day, private coaching session at Coronet Peak or The Remarkables, designed to get prepared for This platform allows our teams to: heli-accessed off-piste terrain. • Educate guests about safe skiing Then, they take to the skies for up to two days and riding practices of private heli-skiing, accompanied by a coach and expert guides. Packages include up to three • Recognise and reward positive actions participants (larger groups by request). Ideal for intermediate to advanced skiers. • Address and penalise unsafe behaviour when necessary For bookings and details, contact vipconcierge@ nzski.com or visit https://shop.coronetpeak.co.nz/ Each interaction is logged against individual guest shop/product/407 profiles, which staff can access in real time while on the slopes. As the programme rolls out in 2026, Coronet Peak will initially focus on a select number of high-priority areas, officially designated as Safe Zones. These targeted locations are designed to encourage safer, more enjoyable experiences for everyone. coronetpeak.co.nz/mountain

Coronet Peak, located in Queenstown – New Zealand, was the country’s first commercial ski area, providing good times since 1947. The mountain is Queenstown’s urban playground, only 20 minutes from downtown along a sealed road. Guests are treated to freshly groomed trails during First Tracks from 8am daily, right through to Night Skiing on select nights until 9pm. Coronet Peak has 280ha of skiable terrain, 1649m elevation with 462m vertical drop. An extensive state-of-the-art snowmaking set up (the largest in the Southern Hemisphere) ensures peak conditions throughout the whole season. Coronet Peak is committed to Kaitiakitanga, guardianship over the mountain. There is a strong programme of activity to restore and enhance the environment including work to eradicate wilding, non-native plants, revegetating trails, and pest control. In addition to a jam-packed winter, summer operations include sightseeing, with the Peak Gondola and chairlift accessed mountain bike trails.

TRANSPORT 1.99m per annum (excl. snowmaking). A dedicated bus service operates throughout the ACCESS 16km sealed road, just 20 minutes day from the centre of Queenstown. As well as this from Queenstown. a night time service is available for Night Ski events. TERRAIN approx. 14% beginner, 33% intermediate, 25% advanced, 27% expert.
ANTICIPATED CLOSING DATE 4 October NON SKIERS A sledding area and Gondola assisted access to the top of the mountain.

VERTICAL DROP 462m SNOWSPORTS Private and group coaching available SKIABLE AREA 280 hectares as well as high performance camps and adaptive programmes. KIDS PROGRAMMES Skiwiland Childcare for 2-5 year

olds. Group or private lessons for ages 5-16 years. Plus Coronet Peak has the most extensive snowmaking a new Groms 3-day Midweek Camp for 4-12 year olds. setup in the Southern Hemisphere with a total of 229 fixed guns, and three portable guns for reliable FOOD & BEVERAGE Four distinct outlets. West Wing snow coverage top to bottom. eatery serves hot buffet breakfast from 7.30am* before transitioning to Mountain Meals, Pizza and Noodle

offerings. Plus the convenient Grab n Go, Ice Bar One high-speed six-seater chair, one high-speed six- and Heidis. seater and gondola cabins, one high-speed quad chair, one T-bar and four surface conveyor lifts. RENTALS Full Head & Salomon recreational and performance rental gear available as well as on mountain workshop featuring the latest Montana tuning robot for equipment.

Coronet Peak belongs to family owned and SUPPORT FOR CONSERVATION: operated business – NZSki. Our sister mountains Planting, revegetation and pest control targets are across the valley at The Remarkables and remain in place at each mountain, and we now further north at Methven’s Mt Hutt. have some AI traps in use. In 2025 we began supporting Southern Lakes Sanctuary alongside At NZSki our primary greenhouse gas emission our continuous support of the Kea Conservation sources are diesel fuel - used for grooming, Trust. transport and building heating - and electricity. Together our three mountains produce RENEWABLE ELECTRICITY: approximately 3000 Tonnes of greenhouse gas We’ve partnered with Meridian Energy to use their emissions (Scope 1 and 2 only) annually. Certified Renewable Energy product. This allows us to report our mountain market-based Scope 2 HOW WE’RE MAKING emissions as zero.*
We’ve purchased carbon credits from Climate
WASTE REDUCTION: Action Company (formerly Carbonz). Since 2022

With continued use in 2025, we saved as per the GHG Protocol’s Scope 2 Guidance. 207k units of single-use food trays from landfill. To find out more visit our Sustainable Slopes page.
